name: ratified decisions (ADR index)
description: apps/web is a thin Next.js App Router shell — web-only, logic stays extraction-ready in packages; RSC + Server Actions, TanStack Query where interactive (ADR-0002, ADR-0008).

name: onboarding-wiring
description: apps/web wires Creator Onboarding as a thin shell: browser Better Auth client (magicLink+emailOTP) for the cookie sign-in flow; useChat(DefaultChatTransport) maps UIMessage<->InterviewMessage via prepareSendMessagesRequest so the interview route Zod-validates one shape; profile-gen runs via runAgentStructured with a RESONANCE_FAKES-only injected canned tool-calling model (ai/test), dynamically imported to stay out of prod; commit redirects to the returned profileId because getCreatorProfileById is a profile-PK lookup with no by-user query exposed.

name: web-live-by-default-di
description: apps/web is live-by-default (ADR-0018): Server Actions call the live @resonance/ai seam (runAgentStructured with no injected model -> resolveModel; commit ctx passes resolveEmbedder()). No RESONANCE_FAKES runtime branch and no dynamic import of ai/test in shipped code. Tests inject fakes ONLY via DI: actions.test.ts mocks the @resonance/ai public entrypoint (getSession/runAgentStructured/commitCreatorProfile/resolveEmbedder), so unit tests stay green with zero credentials without ever hitting a live path or the removed env flag. The runner's RunInput.model DI seam stays open but is taken by tests only. The E2E OTP harness (app/api/test/last-otp + e2e/*) is a SEPARATE human-gated seed (resonance-a4a4) and still uses RESONANCE_FAKES on purpose.

name: usechat-onerror-surface
description: Surface live streaming failures in the interview UI (AI SDK v6 / @ai-sdk/react). useChat's onError callback sets a streamError state; the client renders an inline, token-only error card (border-danger + text-danger on bg-surface, role=alert) with a 'Try again' Button (variant=outline size=sm) that calls useChat's regenerate() to re-run the last turn and clears the error. A fresh user turn also clears streamError before the new stream starts. Without this, a resolveModel throw or Gateway failure vanishes and reads as a silent no-response. RTL test pattern: since useChat is mocked, capture the options via vi.mocked(useChat).mock.calls.at(-1)?.[0], narrow-cast to read onError (it lives on the ChatInit arm of the UseChatOptions union, so direct property access fails typecheck), fire it inside act(), then assert the alert renders and retry calls regenerate + clears the surface.
